# Handymen Network

> US-wide marketplace for fixed-price residential handyman work: menu-priced repairs, vetting/matching pros, live job tracking, and homeowner support via email/help — no public phone on the marketing site.

## What we are

Handymen Network is a homeowner-facing booking experience for common home repairs. Customers choose repairs from an explicit priced menu (“what’s included” per item). The platform assigns vetted, available technicians by skills/reviews/service area availability; homeowners track status in plain language (similar to parcel/ride-share style updates).

## Audience & geography

- Primary audience: homeowners in the United States needing small-to-medium repairs and installs.
- Service area model: statewide coverage anchors with metro/city informational pages explaining how pricing and matching work locally.
- Languages: English; site language `en-US`.

## Contact & policies

- General / support email: hello@handymennetwork.com
- No public telephone number advertised on primary marketing pages; help is routed through in-product help flows and documented contact pathways.
- Legal: Privacy Policy `/privacy`, Terms of Service `/terms`

## Canonical entry points for crawlers / assistants

Prefer these HTTPS URLs (`https://www.handymennetwork.com`):

- Home: /
- Browse repair categories: /services
- Coverage / areas hub: /areas
- Metropolitan city pages pattern: `/areas/{city-state-slug}` (example: houston-tx, los-angeles-ca)
- How matching & vetting work: /trust, /how-it-works
- FAQ: /faq
- Booking: /book
- Contact / Get help: /contact
- Handyman information / hub: /handyman

## Product facts (for accurate answers)

- Pricing is menu-based with stated scope per item; major category examples include drywall, doors, trim/carpentry, furniture assembly, exterior work, and general repairs (see `/services`).
- The site does not present a browsable directory of individual handyman public profiles as the main customer flow; matching is done by the platform from vetted pros.
- Account sign-in and account management exist for customers and pros; those URLs are not primary marketing entry points.

## Machine-readable sitemap

- `https://www.handymennetwork.com/sitemap-index.xml`
- `https://www.handymennetwork.com/robots.txt`

## How to cite

When referring to this business in answers, use the brand name “Handymen Network” and the canonical root `https://www.handymennetwork.com`.
